label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. So first, I need to figure out what today's date is. Then, subtract one month from that date to find the answer. Alright, the last day of February depends on whether it's a leap year. Let me check if 2001 is a leap year. Leap years are divisible by 4, but not by 100 unless also by 400. 2001 divided by 4 is 500.25, so not a whole number. So 2001 is not a leap year. That means the last day of February 2001 is February 28, 2001. So Jane was born on 02/28/2001. Now, today is her 16th birthday. So we need to add 16 years to her birthdate. Let me calculate that. 2001 + 16 = 2017. So her 16th birthday is on 02/28/2017. So today is 02/28/2017. The question asks for the date a month ago from today. So subtract one month from 02/28/2017. Subtracting one month would take us to January 28, 2017. Let me check if that's correct. If today is February 28, then a month ago would be January 28. Because February is the second month, so one month prior would be January. So that would be 01/28/2017. Looking at the options, option D is 01/28/2017. So the answer should be D. Let me double-check. Jane's birthday is on 02/28 every year. In 2017, her 16th birthday would be 02/28/2017. A month before that is 01/28/2017. Yes, that seems right. The other options don't fit.
